title: FollowData
description:
`follow` (enrollment + first-receive). Each offered skill is a TOFU offer, never auto-landed.
**INFERRED** (additive-only). All disclosure fields are optional, so an old consumer ignores
them.

currency.description:
The auto-update-trigger outcome, present when completing the enrollment armed the session-start hook
(a pure follower never runs `add`, so enrollment is where their auto-update gets armed — best-effort:
a degraded config edit is disclosed here, never a rolled-back enrollment).

pending.description:
Present when `follow` returned a pending device-authorization that needs a human verification step
(the client's two-call enrollment surface — visit the URL, then re-run `follow`).

plane_base_url.description:
The API base URL this machine enrolled against (disclosed from the protocol card — a share
address may ride another host; this is where the device actually dials).

skills.description: First-receive offers — empty when the link is membership-only.

triggers.description:
The breadth arming sweep's outcomes — one row per OTHER detected agent whose auto-update
trigger was armed alongside the active adapter's (`currency` above). Honest per agent:
`state`/`currency_kind` follow the same evidence rules every trigger report does, and
`note` names the consent step still owed (or the docs-level evidence caveat). **Additive.**

workspace_display_name.description: The workspace display name (disclosed at enrollment).

BreadthTriggerReport.description:
One breadth-sweep trigger outcome for a DETECTED registry agent (beyond the active adapter's
own [`crate::TriggerReport`]). `agent` is the registry slug; the state/kind pair follows the
same honesty rule everywhere: only `active` carries a live kind, everything else advertises
the explicit-pull floor. **Additive.**

note.description: The consent step still owed, or the evidence-level caveat — `None` when nothing needs saying.

touched_path.description: The config file the (un)install edited, when it edited one.

CurrencyKind.description:
What fires the update check for a harness — drives HONEST receipt copy ("current by next session",
"within about a minute", …). `ExplicitPullOnly` is the honest-degrade floor.

oneOf.description:
A session-boundary hook runs the sweep (Claude Code's SessionStart; Hermes's
`on_session_start`/`on_session_reset`) — updates are current when a session begins.

A harness-scheduled recurring job runs the sweep (OpenClaw's silent cron) — updates land
within the cadence while the harness's scheduler is running.
No automatic trigger is provably live — updates land on an explicit `topos update`.

EnrollmentPending.description:
A pending device-authorization a `follow` surfaced — the human visits `verification_uri_complete` (which
embeds the `user_code`), then the client re-polls. **INFERRED.**

expires_at.description: The session expiry as an RFC-3339 string, if it expires.

interval_secs.description: The minimum poll interval, in seconds — a headless agent re-invokes no faster than this.

user_code.description:
The short human-facing code embedded in `verification_uri_complete` (a cross-check against the
approval page — the human clicks the URL; the code is never typed as a secret).

verification_uri_complete.description: The verification URL with the `user_code` embedded — the human opens it to approve the session.

FollowOffer.description: A single skill offered at `follow` — disclosed, awaiting a direct human yes (TOFU). **INFERRED.**

Offer.description:
The re-disclosed bytes a `pull` offers (confirm-each / first-receive). **INFERRED fields** — the
spec pins that the offer re-discloses + re-binds the digest, not its exact shape.

TriggerReport.description:
The result of installing a auto-update trigger — what was touched + the marker, so re-install is
idempotent (the marker is a managed sentinel block in the config edit, NEVER in skill bytes).

marker_id.description: `topos` + harness id + schema version + command identity.

touched_path.description: The config file edited (never a skill dir).

oneOf.description: An equivalent command exists without our marker — adopt-or-leave, never blind-append.
